content:
# Design Decision: Database Choice

## When to Use PostgreSQL
- Complex queries with joins
- ACID compliance required
- Relational data with foreign keys
- Full-text search needs
- JSON/JSONB for flexible schemas

## When to Use MongoDB
- Document-oriented data
- Flexible/evolving schemas
- High write throughput
- Geospatial queries
- Rapid prototyping

## When to Use Redis
- Caching layer
- Session storage
- Real-time leaderboards
- Rate limiting
- Pub/sub messaging

## When to Use SQLite
- Development/testing
- Small applications
- Embedded systems
- Single-user apps
- File-based storage

## Decision Matrix
```
| Requirement           | PostgreSQL | MongoDB | Redis | SQLite |
|-----------------------|------------|---------|-------|--------|
| Complex queries       | ✓✓✓        | ✓       | ✗     | ✓✓     |
| Horizontal scaling    | ✓          | ✓✓✓     | ✓✓✓   | ✗      |
| ACID transactions     | ✓✓✓        | ✓✓      | ✓     | ✓✓     |
| Flexible schema       | ✓✓         | ✓✓✓     | ✓✓✓   | ✓      |
| Real-time             | ✓          | ✓       | ✓✓✓   | ✗      |
| Easy setup            | ✓          | ✓✓      | ✓✓    | ✓✓✓    |
```

## Recommended Stack for BharatBuild
- **Primary DB**: PostgreSQL (structured data)
- **Cache**: Redis (sessions, caching)
- **Search**: PostgreSQL full-text or Elasticsearch

# Design Decision: Authentication Method

## JWT (JSON Web Tokens)
### Pros
- Stateless (no server storage)
- Scalable across servers
- Contains user data in payload
- Works well with microservices

### Cons
- Cannot revoke easily
- Larger than session ID
- Must handle token refresh

### Use When
- Building APIs for mobile/SPA
- Microservices architecture
- Need stateless authentication

## Session-based Authentication
### Pros
- Easy to revoke
- Smaller cookie size
- Server controls session data

### Cons
- Requires session storage
- Harder to scale
- Server must look up session

### Use When
- Traditional web apps
- Need instant logout
- Single server deployment

## OAuth 2.0 / Social Login
### Pros
- No password management
- Users trust providers
- Reduced friction

### Cons
- Dependent on providers
- Complex implementation
- Privacy concerns

### Use When
- Consumer apps
- Want social features
- Reduce signup friction

## Recommended Implementation
```python
# JWT with refresh token strategy
ACCESS_TOKEN_EXPIRE = 15  # minutes (short-lived)
REFRESH_TOKEN_EXPIRE = 7  # days (long-lived)

# Store refresh tokens in database for revocation
class RefreshToken(Base):
    id = Column(String, primary_key=True)
    user_id = Column(String, ForeignKey("users.id"))
    token_hash = Column(String)  # Hashed token
    expires_at = Column(DateTime)
    revoked = Column(Boolean, default=False)
```

# Design Decision: API Architecture

## REST vs GraphQL

### REST
#### Pros
- Simple and well-understood
- Cacheable (HTTP caching)
- Stateless
- Wide tooling support

#### Cons
- Over-fetching/under-fetching
- Multiple endpoints
- Versioning challenges

#### Use When
- CRUD operations
- Public APIs
- Caching is important
- Team is REST-experienced

### GraphQL
#### Pros
- Fetch exactly what you need
- Single endpoint
- Strong typing
- Great for complex UIs

#### Cons
- Caching is complex
- Learning curve
- N+1 query problem
- Overkill for simple APIs

#### Use When
- Complex data relationships
- Mobile apps (bandwidth)
- Rapidly changing requirements
- Multiple client types

## REST API Versioning
```
# URL versioning (recommended)
GET /api/v1/users
GET /api/v2/users

# Header versioning
GET /api/users
Accept: application/vnd.api+json;version=1

# Query parameter
GET /api/users?version=1
```

## Recommended: REST with OpenAPI
```python
from fastapi import FastAPI

app = FastAPI(
    title="BharatBuild API",
    version="1.0.0",
    openapi_url="/api/v1/openapi.json",
    docs_url="/api/v1/docs",
)

# Auto-generates:
# - OpenAPI schema
# - Swagger UI
# - ReDoc
# - Client SDKs
```

## API Design Checklist
- [ ] Use nouns for resources (/users not /getUsers)
- [ ] Use HTTP methods correctly (GET, POST, PUT, DELETE)
- [ ] Return appropriate status codes
- [ ] Include pagination for lists
- [ ] Version your API
- [ ] Document with OpenAPI
